overview: Removes the RTF layer from text-based input files, outputting only the visible text content. Formatting other than spaces, line-breaks, and tabs is discarded, along with hidden/deleted text, images, and special content. Commonly used to post-process Trados-Tagged RTF files, extracting only the translated sections.

bomOnUTF8.help:
Adds a Byte-Order-Mark (BOM) at the beginning of the output file when the output encoding is UTF-8. Has no effect for non-UTF-8 encodings.

See the [Unicode BOM FAQ](http://www.unicode.org/faq/utf_bom.html) for details on when a BOM is appropriate.
notes: Only applies when the output encoding is UTF-8 — ignored for other encodings.

updateEncoding.help:
Automatically updates encoding declarations in the output file to match the actual output encoding. Supports two formats:

- **XML files**: Updates the `encoding="..."` attribute in the XML declaration. If the declaration exists but has no encoding attribute, one is added. If the file has no XML declaration at all, nothing is changed.
- **HTML files**: Looks for a `content=... charset=...` pattern and updates the `charset` value. If the pattern is not found, nothing is changed.
notes: Warning: The encoding update uses pattern matching, not full XML/HTML parsing. A commented-out declaration could be incorrectly updated.

lineBreak.help: Controls the line-break style used in the output file.
lineBreak.values:
Predefined line-break options:
- `$0d$$0a$` — DOS/Windows: Carriage Return + Line-Feed (`\r\n`, 0x0D+0x0A)
- `$0a$` — Unix/Linux: Line-Feed (`\n`, 0x0A)
- `$0d$` — Macintosh: Carriage-Return (`\r`, 0x0D)

processingNotes: Only the **visible text** of the RTF is output — hidden text, deleted text, images, and other special content are discarded.
All formatting except spaces, line-breaks, and tabs is stripped.
A warning is issued for each line where one or more characters cannot be represented in the selected output encoding.
Can be used to post-process **Trados-Tagged RTF** files — only translated sections are output. For access to both source and target text with inline codes, use the Trados-Tagged RTF Filter instead.
limitations: Characters encoded as `SYMBOL` fields (e.g., drawing symbols inserted from Word using fonts like Dingbats or Wingdings) are not output.
Encoding declaration updates for XML and HTML use pattern matching rather than parsing, so commented-out declarations may be incorrectly updated.
